item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A stays near a balanced position throughout: it rejects one-sided sacrifice of individual rights while still defending social order, national unity, and the big picture. Agent B starts with a balanced disagreement and ends more critical of Indonesian policy dominance by efficiency/stability over inclusivity, but this is a modest shift rather than a clear flip. Both agents engage the other's balance/stability/participation claims; no strong directional drift toward either agent is visible from the text.
